ecountgmifs 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • ecountgmifs-introduction

    Auxiliary control for extended count GMIFS fitting

    ecountgmifs.control.Rd

    Auxiliary function for ecountgmifs() fitting. Typically only used when calling ecountgmifs() through its control argument.

    ecountgmifs.control(
  iteration.max = 1e+06,
  epsilon.max = 0.01,
  epsilon.start = 1e-06,
  epsilon.min = .Machine$double.eps,
  tol = 1e-16,
  nlopt.optim.reltol = tol,
  loglik.reltol.cutoff = 0.25,
  state.track.strategy = c("active.set.change", "all.iteration", "every.k.iteration",
    "none"),
  state.track.freq = 10
)

    Arguments

    iteration.max

    maximum number of path-following iteration steps

    epsilon.max

    maximum step size, e.g. upper bound for step doubling

    epsilon.start

    initial step size before halving procedure

    epsilon.min

    minimum step size, e.g. everything smaller considered a zero step and considered converged

    tol

    numerical convergence tolerance for main C++ code

    nlopt.optim.reltol

    relative tolerance for internal C++ numerical optimization performed using the NLopt library through nloptr.

    loglik.reltol.cutoff

    numeric. Non-negative relative log-likelihood cutoff used by stopping or model-selection rules comparing the fitted path to a reference log-likelihood. This quantity corresponds to the pseudo-\(R^2\) threshold used in the accompanying research paper.

    state.track.strategy

    character. Strategy used to track the algorithm state along the stagewise path. The tracked state may include quantities such as the current coefficient vector, active set, step size, dispersion parameter, elastic-net norm, log-likelihood, and other path diagnostics. One of:

    "active.set.change"

    Track the current state only when the active set changes. Consecutive iterations with the same active set overwrite the previous stored state, so the returned path contains the last iteration associated with each distinct active set. This is the default and usually the most memory-efficient informative option.

    "all.iteration"

    Track the state at every path-following iteration. This gives the most detailed representation of the path, but can require substantial memory for long paths or high-dimensional designs.

    "every.k.iteration"

    Track the state every state.track.freq iterations. This gives a regular subsampling of the path and is useful when full iteration-level tracking is too large.

    "none"

    Do not track the path state. Only final or selected model summaries are returned.

    state.track.freq

    integer. Tracking frequency used when state.track.strategy = "every.k.iteration". For example, state.track.freq = 10 stores the algorithm state every 10 iterations. Ignored by the other tracking strategies.

    Value

    A list of control parameters.

    Details

    The control argument of ecountgmifs() is passed to the internal fitting routine, which uses its elements to control the stagewise path, adaptive step-size procedure, internal numerical optimization, stopping rules, and coefficient-path storage. The function ecountgmifs.control() provides default values and performs basic sanity checking before fitting begins.

    The state-tracking strategy given by state.track.strategy controls the amount of path information stored in the returned ecountgmifs() object. Tracked quantities include the coefficient vector, active set, step size, dispersion parameter, elastic-net norm, log-likelihood, and other diagnostics. Tracking every iteration gives the most detailed representation of the algorithm path, whereas "active.set.change" stores a compressed path indexed by changes in the active set.

    Contents

    Developed by Alikhan Anuarbekov, Jiří Kléma.
